body.title: [sprint] 4 inference tests fail without live sources — add skip-guard or fix routing assertions
body.description:
**Problem (pre-existing, found during T-corpus-visibility-gaps 2026-07-05):** 4 tests in tests/inference/ fail on CLEAN HEAD (verified c8c53a2b via worktree) when no live inference source (ollama/ollama_cloud/hex/google-free) is up — unrelated to any code change:
- test_dicksimnel.py::TestORCostGate::test_worker_google_free_preferred_over_openrouter_when_available
- test_dicksimnel_live_smoke.py::test_dicksimnel_builds_on_hex (HANGS ~2min waiting for live Hex, then fails)
- test_mini_rack.py::test_rules_worker_routes_to_openrouter
- test_mini_rack.py::test_rules_batch_routes_to_local_ollama_at_night

Root cause: sources unavailable → rules engine last-resort-routes to openrouter, so routing-assertion tests fail; the live_smoke test needs a real Hex. In a sandbox these are false-negatives, not regressions.

**Proposed shape:** mark the live/source-dependent ones with a skip-guard (pytest.mark.skipif on source availability) so a sourceless run is green; keep the routing-logic assertions runnable by injecting a mock available source registry (as test_mini_rack partially does) rather than depending on ambient availability. The live_smoke test should skip fast (not hang 2min) when Hex is unreachable.

**Affected files:** tests/inference/test_dicksimnel.py, tests/inference/test_dicksimnel_live_smoke.py, tests/inference/test_mini_rack.py (+ possibly a shared conftest source-availability fixture).

**Design rules:** integration tests hit real Postgres (no mocks) — but source AVAILABILITY is environment, not logic; guarding on it is legitimate. Don't weaken the routing assertions themselves.

**Scope boundary:** make a no-live-source run green (skip or mock); do NOT change rules-engine routing behavior.

**Validation:** `pytest tests/inference/ -q` on a box with no ollama/hex is green (0 failed; skips allowed).

**Completion criteria:** the 4 named tests either pass via injected mock sources or skip cleanly (no hang) when sources are down.

**Test plan:** run the suite with all live sources down — assert 0 failures. No new proof node; this is test-hygiene (shipped-unproven if no red form: the lever is a source-availability fixture).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
